checks.a_analysis: The output title and text segments correspond to the top-level heading in the source PDF. No fabricated text was found. The image alt text and caption are expected AI additions.
There is no contact information (phone, email, URL) in the source PDF or the output JSON.
The single page of the document is represented in the output.
The source document does not contain any tables; the legend is formatted as a key for the map graphic.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The output fails to capture a significant amount of text content from the source. Specifically, the legend which provides the critical mapping for 'Phase 1 (2020)', 'Phase 2 (2021)', etc., is entirely missing from the textual elements. Additionally, over 100 county names displayed on the map, the status date, and the footer metadata (scale, spatial reference) are omitted from the machine-readable text. The output word count of 4 represents a loss of over 90% of the textual information present in the source document.
checks.b_evidence_source: Legend text (Phase 1 (2020), Phase 2 (2021), Phase 3 (2022), Phase 4 (2023), Not Started (2010 Data), In Progress, Initial QC Complete, SFHA Update, Needs Review); County names (e.g., Cherokee, Clay, Macon...); Footer metadata (Scale: 1:3,179,372, Spatial Reference Name, etc.); Status date (Status as of: 6/16/2025).
checks.b_evidence_output: The text elements only contain the document title: 'Building Footprint Update Status'. All legend and map-specific text is missing from the elements array.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Since the vast majority of text was omitted (captured in C5), there are no specific wording changes to evaluate for remaining text as the title matches perfectly.

checks.a_analysis: The document title 'Building Footprint Update Status' appears as the metadata title and the primary section heading. This is a standard mapping for structural metadata and content headings and does not constitute content duplication within the body text. No other text blocks are repeated.

The output identifies one primary heading ('Building Footprint Update Status'), which correctly represents the top-level title of the source document. No lower-level headings are skipped as there are no subsequent text headings identified in the structure; the sub-title 'Risk Building Footprint Refresh' is appropriately handled as part of the image content/caption.
The source document does not contain any data tables. The map and its legend are graphical elements correctly encapsulated in an image element.
The reading order in the output follows the logical top-to-bottom flow of the source document: starting with the main title and proceeding to the central infographic (image).

There are no text-based lists in the source document. The legend information is part of the integrated map graphic and is correctly described in the image alt-text rather than being forced into a list structure.
No links are present in the source document, and no links have been injected into the JSON output.

checks.a_analysis: The source PDF is organized around a large, central map showing the update status across North Carolina. The HTML output completely omits this central map, rendering only the document title and a logo that was originally in the footer of the source document. The visual organization is fundamentally broken because the primary content of the page (the map and its legend) is missing, leaving only a caption and an unrelated logo in its place.
checks.b_evidence_source: A full-page layout featuring a large map of North Carolina titled 'Risk Building Footprint Refresh' with a legend, scale, and multiple agency logos.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML displays the main title 'Building Footprint Update Status' followed by a single 'EMAP Accredited' logo and a caption 'Risk Building Footprint Refresh'. The entire map and its associated spatial context are gone.

checks.a_analysis: There are no tables in the source PDF document; the data is presented via a map and legend.

The most significant meaningful image in the source PDF—the 'Risk Building Footprint Refresh' map—is completely missing from the HTML output. Additionally, the agency logos (DPS header logo, NC DPS footer logo, and NC Emergency Management logo) are also missing, with the exception of the EMAP logo which has been misplaced. The map, legend, north arrow, and scale bar are critical visual information that did not transition to the HTML.
checks.b_evidence_source: The central map image showing North Carolina counties colored by status, including the legend, north arrow, and scale bar.
checks.b_evidence_output: No map image or legend is present in the HTML screenshot.
checks.b_evidence_location: Page 1, central and bottom content areas

checks.a_analysis: There is no evidence of body content duplication in the HTML output.
